This is a picture talking about air leak.
I spoke to you that in some instances, endobronchial valves are used in patients with chronic air leak.
So what you do is you do a bronchoscopy and you put in a balloon catheter.
These are patients with a chest tube which is bubbling.
So when you've got a patient with a chest tube which is bubbling, you don't know where is the air leak coming from.
So you go into various segments of the lung and start blocking it off.
If the air leak stops, you know that is the segment which is leaking air and you deploy a valve into that segment and that will cause atelectasis of the segment distal.
Atelectasis will allow the air leak area to heal, the bronchopleural fistula to heal and then once
the drain is out and the patient is better, then you can go in and take the valve out after about six to eight weeks and then the lung will re-expand.
Now, this is not for LVRs.
This is just an additional information I wanted to give you for use of a valve for air leak, okay?
The pulmonologist are also using a technology called as vapor ablation therapy.
So this is another technology that's being used and it is showing some promising benefits in bronchoscopic lung volume reduction.
